entry.title: Re: How to Block a Ship-to Location in SAP ByDesign

entry.title: Re: Safety Stock Allocation from Stock Segmented Stock
entry.content: A better tag for this question would be 'MM Inventory Management'. This is not a 'SAP HANA' issue.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Copy Data to specific Dimension Elements
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a Dimension with the following elements: A, A1, B, B1, C, C1. It's a public dimension with properties.

For the master data of Dimension Elements A, B, and C, we have entries in the properties:

A = A1, B = B1, C = C1

We also have transactional data for A, B, and C, and we want to copy these values to their corresponding counterparts, which are specified in the properties.

Does anyone have an idea on how to set up an advanced formula for this without hard-coding, such as:

DATA(A1)=RESULTLOOKUP(A)

I'm looking for a more dynamic solution. Any suggestions?

Kind regards,

Ismail

entry.title: Re: crowd partner admin
entry.content:
Hello aorkunuyar 

No i didn't change it , because when i click on the edit button the crowd type disappear so i can not change the type.

entry.title: Can you trust Pam?
entry.content:
PAM—The SAP Product Availability Matrix—provides details about SAP software product versions, including availability and end-of-maintenance dates, upgrade paths, and technical release information (database platforms, Java platforms, operating systems, and so on). The information is 100% trustworthy for current releases.   But ....the PAM is not complete—it does not describe the strategy that SAP will follow—and a 2027 End of Maintenance Date does mean the product is end of life in 2027.  I found that many colleagues and customers could not find the information so I made a list. This isn't complete or official. If something is out of date or missing please post it in the comments. 

  • Release and Maintenance Strategy: https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/release-upgrade-maintenance/maintenance-strategy/sap-release-and-maintenance-strategy-new.pdf 
  • Commitment for SAP S/4HANA until 2040:https://support.sap.com/en/release-upgrade-maintenance/maintenance-information/maintenance-strategy/s4hana-business-suite7.html 
  • BI: SAP Analytics Business Intelligence Statement of D... - SAP Community
  • Data Services: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/statement-of-direction-sap-data-services-as-of-july-2024/ba-p/13762644
  • Information Steward: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/statement-of-direction-sap-information-steward/ba-p/13795111 
  • PowerDesigner: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3280082 
  • Cloud Integration For Data Services (IBP Integration):  SAP Cloud Integration for data services - Product ... - SAP Community
  • Neo: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/farewell-neo-sap-btp-multi-cloud-environment-the-deployment-environment-of/ba-p/13560080 
  • GRC: GRC Tuesdays: Announcing SAP’s plans for a next generation Governance, Risk, and Compliance Platform: SAP GRC edition for SAP HANA | SAP Blogs
  • BPC: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-business-planning-and-consolidation-sap-bpc-strategy/ba-p/13512347
  • Process Integration: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/process-integration-pi-way-forward-and-recommended-actions/ba-p/13481628 
  • HR and Payroll: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-human-capital-management-for-sap-s-4hana-now-available-as-part-of-the/ba-p/13405101

entry.title: Re: Resource scheduling app- Order operation is confirmed...
entry.content:
Hello! Have you consulted our SAP Help documentation on the status and when an order operation is completed here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/8308e6d301d54584a33cd04a9861bc52/79c52c17a7c147b18035d8c4679a5bd6.html?locale=en-US ?
Best regards,
Markus

entry.title: Re: Bring the data from Azure DataBricks (ADB) to SAP ABA...

entry.title: ABAP CDS Parameter filter Value Help results with entity field
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

I'm trying to develop a RAP application with an action to change the status that has as an input parameter to specify the status.

The parameter abstract entity is like that:

define abstract entity ZD_ChangeStatusP
{

  @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{
      entity: {
          name: 'ZI_DocumentStatus',
          element: 'Status'
      },
      
      additionalBinding: [{
          localElement: '',
          element: 'DocumentType',
          usage: #RESULT
      }]
  }]
  Status : zdocument_status;

}

I'd like to filter the results with the "DocumenType" value from the entity.

Is it possible?

Thanks in advance!

Carsten

entry.title: Re: End-to-end Processes and modular processes in SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition
entry.content:
hi @christianoehler 

I trust all is well!
I got your contact from Jan Matthes. 

I placed this analysis and some questions in the comments in this blog, and I wanted to find out if you could provide me with some additional feedback based on the analysis I have done.

The context is , from the point of view of an Organization, and how the organize their Process Owners and Business Analysits to resolve either Process Intelligence investigations or Process Insights Initaitves and Actions, many times such Process Owners wont be at the Modular Process level, they are organized at lower leves in the Process decomposition hierarchy.

This my question is related to understanding how are the Metrics /PPIs allocated between (as in my example: Secure to Pay and Plan to Full, when they are duplicated in the Modular process landing page, as in the screen shot above)

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Reference Fields Mapping in CGI Payment Formats
entry.content:
Hi,

Has anyone got clue how to populate REF++ fields? Do we need to use Event 05?

Thanks & Regards.

entry.title: How to read SAP routing information from production order
entry.content:
Hi 
I want to get details of tasks list group and group counter of routing

I am using these API but i don't find any interrelated property to connect production order and production routing 

API i am using
production order API : https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_PRODUCTION_ORDER_2_SRV_0001/path/get_A_ProductionOrder_2
Production routing : https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_PRODUCTION_ROUTING_0001/path/get_ProductionRoutingHeader_ProductionRoutingGroup___ProductionRoutingGroup___ProductionRouting___ProductionRouting___ProductionRoutingInternalVers___ProductionRoutingInternalVers___

And how to get this values for routing API from production order
ProductionRoutingGroup
ProductionRouting
ProductionRoutingInternalVers


Thank you,
Onkar Gavali

entry.title: Brick by Brick: A Journey of Travel, Photography, and LEGO Love
Re: Extract Salesforce data into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence using SAP Integration Suite
entry.content: Nice Blogpost @hoangvu  !

entry.title: ALTERNATIVE RESOURCE
entry.content:
Hi SAP expert, 

I have a question, the context is the following. 

I have :

- a product A which should has as alternative resource 1&2&3

- a product B which should has as alternative resource 1&3

- a product C which should has as alternative resource 3

These products share the same recipe. Is any way to manage differents alternative resource depending of the products if they have the same recipe ?

Thank you in advance for your help.

Nicolas

entry.title: ALV Display - Custom Column Name - cl_salv_table
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

Any idea on the reason why this is not working. I can't get the custom column name in the report dispaly.

 


TYPES: BEGIN OF gty_data,

matnr TYPE matnr, " Material Number

sernr TYPE gernr, " Serial Number

pin TYPE string, " PIN (Assuming PIN as a string)

END OF gty_data,

gty_tab_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF gty_data.

 

 

METHOD display_data.

TRY.

cl_salv_table=>factory( IMPORTING r_salv_table = DATA(lr_alv)

CHANGING t_table = ct_data ).

CATCH cx_salv_msg.

ENDTRY.

TRY.

lr_alv->get_layout( )->set_key( VALUE #( report = sy-repid ) ).

lr_alv->get_layout( )->set_default( abap_true ).

lr_alv->get_layout( )->set_save_restriction( if_salv_c_layout=>restrict_none ).

lr_alv->get_functions( )->set_all( abap_true ).

lr_alv->get_columns( )->get_column( 'pin' )->set_long_text( 'PIN' ).

lr_alv->get_columns( )->get_column( 'pin' )->set_medium_text( 'PIN' ).

lr_alv->get_columns( )->get_column( 'pin' )->set_short_text( 'PIN' ).

CATCH cx_salv_not_found.

ENDTRY.

CALL METHOD lr_alv->display.

ENDMETHOD.



Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Purchase order business scenario
entry.content: looks like you need to do reversal of all transection that you did. then you need to update PO before. or else you can provide credit note to vendor as well against this PO.

entry.title: Re: ALV Display - Custom Column Name - cl_salv_table
entry.content:
Hello,
There is no warning or syntax error.
The aim was to set custom column name in a single line without using 

DATA: lo_columns TYPE REF TO cl_salv_columns_table,

lo_column TYPE REF TO cl_salv_column.

entry.title: Re: Add tab Customize to Print form pdf in S4 hana public...

entry.title: Re: ALV Display - Custom Column Name - cl_salv_table
entry.content:
First, do yourself and us a favour, and use the insert code feature in the editor (</>).

Second, try to write the column names in uppercase ('PIN')  when you  call the get_column method

 

lr_alv->get_columns( )->get_column( 'PIN' )

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to change time zone on Hana database 2.0 SPS07 to be different than OS
Re: Data Removal
entry.content:
Hi @lesleysherry7775 ,

You could use DTW, just setting up the fields you want to clear with Dash "/".

The example below will clear the field U_remarks for the BP VENDOR0001:

CardCodeU_Remarks
CardCodeU_Remarks
VENDOR0001/

Please note that this will work mostly with free text fields. Depending on the data you wish to clear out, this might not help, but at least you can give a try.

Best regards,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to change time zone on Hana database 2...
entry.content:
Based on 3348463 - Time differencies between the Database and application server, I don't think it is possible. 

"The OS time and SAP system time must be in sync otherwise there will be frequent runtime errors."

entry.title: Re: ALV Display - Custom Column Name - cl_salv_table
entry.content:
Hey,

Thanks, Worked.

New to the community,  will use the code feature in the editor (</>) from now on.

entry.title: Re: What Are You Looking Forward to Learning or Doing at Devtoberfest This Year?
entry.content: I’m eager to learn how new technologies can drive value for companies. It’s one thing to implement a technology, but it’s another to understand its broader impact on business strategy and performance.

entry.title: Re: how to set up PMR automatically when Production order is released?
entry.content: as far as i know, you need to generate PMR manually only after releasing Pro order. But if you want to do automatic good issue you can do that after releasing the order. just used the backflush function hear.

But not sure is a good idea... several SAP notes related to search help:

Miguel Angel.

entry.title: SAP STAMMTISCH KOLKATA - 31st August,2024
entry.content:
Starting from this year, we are about to host minimum of four events per year(we are planning to increase this number), including:

- At least one topic-focused event

- One networking event

This event is more than just a meetup; it's a celebration of our collective passion for SAP and the incredible growth of our local community. Whether you're an experienced SAP professional, a business thriving on SAP solutions, or someone eager to dive into the SAP world, this event is for you!

 

**Why You Should Attend:**

 

- **Inspiring Discussions**: Dive deep into the growth of our local SAP community and business landscape.

 

- **Hiring Insights**: Get the latest on SAP hiring trends and discover the skills that are in demand.

 

- **Networking**: Connect with fellow SAP enthusiasts, industry experts, and potential mentors.

 

- **Joyful Conversations**: Enjoy delicious snacks and refreshments while you share experiences and ideas.

 

Kolkata, let's make this Stammtisch a memorable one! Bring your energy, your ideas, and your enthusiasm for a fun-filled and informative afternoon. We can't wait to see you there and make new connections that will drive our community forward.

As our Kolkata Community continues to grow, we encourage collaboration to make it a vibrant, knowledge-sharing community for all! 🌟

Special thanks to -

Laura Schmitz

Christoph Christophersen

 

Sindhu Gangadharan Josh "Bluebeard" Bentley Svea Becker Katherine Kenneally Abhiishek Chatterjee Mahesh Palavalli Shilpa Shankar Sheena MK 

 

 

Lets come together and make this event a huge success...❤️

#SAPKOLKATACOMMUNITY

#SAPCOMMUNITY

##SAPEVENT2024

entry.title: DM_SCHEDULE not displaying the Job End time
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have some import jobs to load fact data in SAC Planning model. We are also monitoring the jobs last run via DM_SCHEDULE model report. This model provided by SAP and it helps to monitor the Data management jobs. It's showing Null for Job end time and job start time. It worked before the SAC Q3 Upgrade. Anyone facing this issue ?

entry.title: SAP Customer Checkout Manager migration to a new server
entry.content:
A German version of the blog post can be found here.

In this blog post, you will learn the basics needed to migrate an SAP Customer Checkout Manager (CCOm) installation to another server. This includes the prerequisites and the fundamental steps to follow.

What information and access are required?

  • Access to both the old and new servers
  • Admin password and hash key from the initial configuration
  • Access to the CCOm database (SAP HANA or MS SQL)
  • Knowledge of the database name/schema (e.g., CCOS)
  • Database user credentials (e.g., Admin user/sa user)
  • The port of the old CCOm installation (e.g., 9090 or 8080)
  • The ccos.war file in the correct version

What steps should be followed?

  • Stop the Apache Tomcat service on the old server
  • Create backups
  • Copy the database
  • Copy the "CustomerCheckout Central Server" folder (e.g., C:\SAP\CustomerCheckout Central Server)
  • Install CCOm on the new server
  • Perform the initial configuration
  • Stop CCOm
  • Replace the database and "CustomerCheckout Central Server" folder
  • Start CCOm
  • Enter new connection data in the POS systems (CCO)

 

Information and Access

First, ensure that the required access and connection data mentioned above are available. Without the admin password and hash key used during the initial configuration, the migration as described here will not be possible. Useful information about the currently used version, database connection, and the database schema can be found in CCOm under Configuration/Information.

Avoid deviations between the old and new installations whenever possible.

 

Procedure

The migration should generally not take place during active operations. Plan sufficient time outside of business hours for the migration. Additionally, it is advisable to test the process in a test environment first.

Start by stopping the CCOm on the old server, which can be done via the services.

Next, a copy of the CCOm database should be made, which can also be deployed on a new server if necessary. A backup of the Apache Tomcat folder (e.g., under C:\Program Files\Apache Software Foundation\Tomcat 9.0_Tomcat9_FP19) should also be created.

The ccos.war file (e.g., C:\Program Files\Apache Software Foundation\Tomcat 9.0_Tomcat9_FP19\webapps\ccos.war) and the "CustomerCheckout Central Server" folder (e.g., C:\SAP\CustomerCheckout Central Server) should be transferred to the new server.

In the next step, the CCOm installation is performed as usual, choosing the same port as in the old installation. The ccos.war file from the old server can be used, or a new file can be downloaded. It is important to choose the same version (same feature pack) as in the CCOm installation on the old server.

 

Initial Configuration

During the initial configuration, the connection data for the corresponding database is selected, and a user with read and write permissions who can create a new schema (e.g., Admin/sa) is entered.

If the "CustomerCheckout Central Server" folder is not replaced, it is not necessarily required to use the same database user as in the original initial configuration. However, since this folder contains, for example, the images of the quick selection, a replacement is recommended. The schema name, on the other hand, must have the same name as in the original installation on the old server (e.g., CCOS).

 

Check the box "Automatically create schema (if non-existent)" to allow the database schema to be recreated initially.

Now use the same admin password and hash key as in the original CCOm installation on the old server:

Click OK and wait until the message appears indicating that the configuration was successful. You can check or confirm that the installation has been carried out correctly by logging in to CCOm.

Now, shut down Apache Tomcat again (e.g., via services), replace the new "SAP Customer Checkout Central Server" folder with the one copied from the old server (if using the same database user as in the initial configuration), and swap out the previously copied database.

Finally, restart CCOm via services and verify after logging in that all data (customers, users, items, receipts, etc.) is available. Don't forget to enter the new connection data in the POS system under Configuration/Integration/SAP Customer Checkout Manager.

entry.title: Re: Link with Rebate and Sales Order (scope item 1B6)
entry.content: Kindly check the Markus comment above, does it clarify your question?

entry.title: Re: Alternate Unit of Measure
entry.content:
Hi Prabhat,

In the Public Cloud, the UoM comes from the price condition, and only one UoM is used and available in that condition. There is no easy workaround that can provide the other UoM. 

I would suggest you create a feature request, please refer to 2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

Best regards,

Troy

entry.title: Plant determination to repoint orders automatically to another plant
entry.content: Is there a way to use plant determination by Ship To/ Sold To, Products and minimun pallet quantity to point orders to another plant rather than manually changing once the order is placed?

entry.title: Re: Migration roadmap from ECC to S4/HANA with two SIDs
entry.content:
Hello @KelberMonteiro,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, multiple instances are not a constraint. There are several reasons why a customer may decide to have more than one productive system, and SAP allows those scenarios.

 

Regards,

Jose M.

Dear Team,

I have received an incident for customer asking 'How to publish the final report to the end users like a PDF in a folder for every month'. Could you please help.

entry.title: Understanding the Current Goal Management Landscape
entry.content:
Leveraging Generative AI in Goal Management

As organizations face challenges with legacy goal-setting methods, such as manual effort, subjective decision-making, and the risk of setting unrealistic or misaligned goals, generative AI offers a transformative approach. Generative AI, a branch of artificial intelligence that creates new content based on existing data, can revolutionize how goals are set, tracked, and achieved within the SuccessFactors PMGM module.

Introduction to Generative AI Goal Management

Generative AI uses advanced algorithms to analyze vast amounts of data, identify patterns, and generate recommendations. AI can optimize the goal-setting process by making it more data-driven, objective, and dynamic. By leveraging AI, organizations can overcome the limitations of traditional methods and create a more agile, efficient, and personalized approach to managing employee performance.

How Generative AI Can Optimize Goal-Setting in SuccessFactors

Data-Driven Goal Setting:
  1. AI-Enhanced Analysis: AI can analyze historical performance data, employee behavior, industry benchmarks, and organizational trends to suggest relevant and achievable goals. Unlike manual goal-setting, which relies heavily on subjective judgment, AI can provide a more objective, data-driven foundation for goal creation.
  2. Automated Goal Generation: AI tools can automatically generate goal recommendations based on an employee’s past performance, skill set, and career aspirations. This reduces the manual effort involved in goal-setting and ensures that goals are aligned with both individual capabilities and organizational objectives.
Personalized Goal Suggestions:
  1. Tailored Goal Recommendations: AI can personalize goal suggestions for each employee, taking into account their unique strengths, areas for improvement, and career growth paths. This personalized approach not only enhances employee engagement but also aligns individual goals with broader organizational priorities.
  2. Adaptive Learning and Development: By analyzing an employee’s performance data and learning history, AI can recommend personalized development plans that support both immediate goal achievement and long-term career growth.

Prerequisite:

  1. Latest Goal Management must be enabled.
  2. IAS/IPS must be enabled
  3. SAP AI Units license.
  4. The following permission needs to be granted in Role-Based Permissions (RBP): 
    • Administrator Permissions → Manage AI Capabilities → AI Services Administration.
    • User Permissions > AI Access > Assisted Performance Goals permission and Assisted Development Goals permission.
  5. End Users must accept the AI Usage Acknowledgment, available from the User Profile menu, to agree on the AI capabilities considerations when it comes to generate outputs.

Generative AI Features in Goal Management

When selecting Create Goal, the Goal Creation Options menu will show a text field where users can provide an input and generate a goal creation using AI technology.

Additionally, the Assisted Writing can be used to get assistance from AI technology to enhance the content from text and textarea fields, like Goal Name, Metric or Description.

The capabilities for the Assisted Writing available in Goal Management are:

  • Enhanced Writing: Suggests enhancements in grammar, spelling, vocabulary choices, and phrasing in the context of the selected line or paragraph.
  • Change Tone: Provides options to change the tone of the writing of the selected line or paragraph to professional, personable, or constructive to suit the communication needs for different audience groups in the organization.
  • Make Shorter: Shortens a selected line or paragraph to convey the message more concisely.
  • Make Longer: Rewrites a selected line or paragraph by adding more words to provide a detailed explanation based on the original content.
  • Create Bulleted List: Converts a selected line or paragraph into a bulleted list to simplify complex information, and enhance readability by breaking it down into easy-to-read points.

Figure 1: AI-Generated Goal Suggestion in SuccessFactors"

The AI-assisted Writing tool offers language reviews only in English.

Note

The AI-assisted Writing tool can be also used in SAP SuccessFactors 360 Reviews, Career Development Planning, Continuous Performance Management, Performance Management and Opportunity Marketplace.

Using Generative AI to Create and Edit Goals

In this example, an employee wants to leverage the Generative AI capabilities to create a relevant and attainable goal based on a user input to "generate more revenue and make customers happy".

Prerequisites

End Users must accept the AI Usage Acknowledgment, available from the User Profile menu, to agree on the AI capabilities considerations when it comes to generate outputs.

Figure 2: Acknowledgement for end user

Steps

  • Navigate to the Goal Plan, using the Home menu or selecting the quick action Manage My Goals from the Home Page.
  • Create a New Goal, selecting Create Goal button.
  • The Goal Creation Options will display different options to create a goal. The first one is the ability to type a message and prompt results from the AI-powered technology. In this example, the employee types generate more revenue and make customers happy. When selecting the Generate button, the AI will prompt a creation of a new goal.

 

  • The new goal is created. You can review the AI-generated content in the edit goal window. The goal fields available for AI generation are Goal Name, Description, Metric, Start and Due dates and Milestones.

  • Further changes can be done, editing the details generated or even use the option to re-generate AI content again and trigger different results that can be easily compared. In the following example, the original goal name generated by AI will be edited using the Generation Input option and change the message prompt.

 

  • Results can be now compared. Notice on the example, how the Generation Input will prompt a different result. User can now accept or discard the new generated results.

 

  • Once the goal is saved, the Goal Details will indicate that this goal is enhanced by AI.

Result

 

Related Links:

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-members/create-personal-goals-using-generative-ai/ba-p/13686751
  • https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/configure-sap-successfactors-performance-and-goals/using-generative-ai-in-goal-management_b5c973b3-8833-4898-8e27-021a6914469a

entry.title: Re: End-to-end Processes and modular processes in SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition
entry.content:
Hi @JD_WongLoera,

Thanks for your detailed question.
I would in fact defer to Jan's good judgement as it is related to choices made for SAP Signavio Process Insights, which wee then automatically cascaded to SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition.

Thanks!
Kind regards

Rene

Nach meinem Verständnis Ihres Anliegens könnte es hilfreicher sein, den vollständigen DOM-Aufbau manuell zu erfassen, anstatt den Bildschirm aufzuzeichnen, um detailliertere Informationen über die Webseiten zu erhalten.

entry.title: Re: SAP ALM Analytics: How to use the SAP Focused RUN REST Advanced Analytics API

Thanks for the info above.

We have issues accessing the Data Providers, the correct Service is activated in SICF (/sap/frun/fi/dp) and a test for the service returns '200 - OK'.

However when trying to access any DP (eg DP_SYSMON) after calling the cUrl, we get '404 Not Found'.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: BPA for ByDesign
entry.content:
Hello Hanu,

Thank you for reaching out to us. It’s clear that you have a well-defined requirement and process in mind. I recommend starting with the pre-built bot that Arun mentioned as a reference. Since we don't have specific guidance on your customized automation, you may find additional support in the Developer tutorial for more information.

Developer Tutorial - https://developers.sap.com/tutorial-navigator.html?tag=software-product%3Atechnology-platform%2Fsap-build%2Fsap-build-process-automation

SBPA Help portal - https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Range prompt parameter hyperlink in Web Intelligence...
entry.content:
Your syntax is incorrect.
Spaces in the prompt Title should be +, not %20

Fiscal Year Range: E.g. (2000 - 2010)

incorrect:

lsRFiscal%20Year%20Range%3A%20E.g.%20(2000%20-%202010)

correct:

lsRFiscal+Year+Range:+E.g.+(2000+-+2010)

see examples on Help Page
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESSOBJECTS_BUSINESS_INTELLIGENCE_PLATFORM/954ffd91a37d45c093d80dd6db2665e4/45f6c4e46e041014910aba7db0e91070.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
Hi @kngupta141 ,

Status 'P' should be mapped to 'O'. This is mentioned in Task 1. Only then, you would get the Status value for all the records. Kindly correct this as it would affect the ongoing tasks.

entry.title: Re: Editing a variable technical name in SAP HANA Studio...
entry.content:
Were you able to edit the technical name on other Studio versions?

You could try to edit the xml file but please do this carefully and at your own risk. Open the "Hana development" perspective -> Open the view with text editor -> To find the underlying XML file. You can find the variable/input parameter entry section <variable> and try to edit there

Right click -> Save and activate the view

entry.title: Re: How to activate a transport request selection bar for the RAP BO of a customizing table

Please open the Item master data to check its Warranty Template under General tab, and make sure that the Duration field of the template is filled out.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model: Introduction to the Dynamic Rules Screen
entry.content:
SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model: Introduction to the Dynamic Rules Screen

As businesses evolve, the ability to adapt and modify business processes swiftly becomes essential. For users of the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Universal Model (SAP PaPM Cloud UM), the Dynamic Rules Screen offers a powerful solution. This tool allows Activity users to maintain and modify business function rules dynamically during runtime without the need for re-activation. This blogpost delves into the key features and functionalities of the Dynamic Rules Screen, highlighting how it streamlines business operations and enhances user experience.

Get to know Dynamic Rules Screen

The Dynamic Rules Screen is designed to provide SAP PaPM Cloud UM Activity users the flexibility to manage rules for business functions directly through the user interface (UI). The key capabilities include:

  • Dynamic Rule Maintenance: Users can edit rules on the fly, without the need to re-activate the underlying data model. Re-activation is only required if there are configuration changes that impact the data model, and this can be performed by an SAP PaPM Cloud user.
  • User-Friendly UI: The interface supports rule editing with the similar convenience features available in well known "Show Screen". This ensures a seamless user experience.
  • Import and Export: The Dynamic Rules, in format of csv, can be easily imported and exported, facilitating maintenance via Excel. This feature is particularly useful for bulk updates and backups.

Key Features of Dynamic Rules Screen

Navigation to the Dynamic Rules Screen

The activity should be in State “Active” and the activity configuration should have the “Show Rules” checkbox selected.

Rules Management - CRUD Operations

The Dynamic Rules Screen offers comprehensive management of rules through a new interface accessible via Show Rules semantic button available in Manage Activity.

Users can perform Create, Read, Update, and Delete (CRUD) operations on rules efficiently.

  • Rules maintenance: Dynamic Rules screen provides a top table for rule management with actions such as creation, editing, deletion, and import/export.

     

  • Input/Result Tables: Located at the bottom of the screen, these tables display the input data and results based on the selected rules. Tables are separated by a tab, users can easily toggle between Input and Result tabs.

  • Result Table Updates: Clicking button "Run" updates the Result table, showing the outcome of applying the rules.

Copy Filters to Rule in Input Grid

This feature enhances rule maintenance by allowing users to apply filters from the Input grid directly to the rules.

  • Enabled Button: When a rule is selected, the "Copy filter to Rule" button becomes active.
  • Applying Filters: Changes in the Input grid filters can be copied to the rule conditions, updating the rules grid accordingly.

Import of Rules

Rules can be imported easily, allowing for quick updates and ensuring consistency across different environments.

Rules are being imported using comments, such as importing data/rules in an activity. As a reference how to import rules, please check documentation on Maintaining Rules > Import Rules.

Export of Rules

Exporting rules as comments enables easy sharing and backup of rules.

Rules are being exported through comments. Once Export Rules is clicked, comment number will be enhanced which indicates that new comment is created.

As a reference how to export rules, please check documentation on Maintaining Rules > Export Rules.

Set Version

The Set Version functionality mirrors the same functionality in the regular "Show Screen", providing users with robust control over changes.

  • Set Version: This feature allows users toof rule configurations, enabling easy rollback to previous states if needed

Erase Rules

The Erase button allows users to remove rules efficiently, simplifying the cleanup of obsolete or incorrect rules.

Group By Rules

When users select the "Group by" option, all rules with the same Rule ID will be grouped together. This grouping simplifies rule management by displaying only the primary rule per Rule ID, specifically the rule that contains an assignment. This way, users can focus on the most relevant rules without being overwhelmed by duplicate entries.

This feature ensures that rule management is more efficient and user-friendly, allowing users to easily identify and work with the key rules within their SAP PaPM Cloud UM environment.

Commenting Feature in Rules Grid

Adding comments to rules enhances collaboration and documentation.

  • Commenting Feature: Located below the rules table, this feature allows users to add, view, and manage comments for each rule, promoting better communication and record-keeping. Added comments will be logged in Manage Comments.

Features in Result Table

Features ensure consistency and control over data management actions in the Dynamic Rules Screen by aligning them with the Manage Activities Screen.

In the Result tab of the Dynamic Rules Screen, specific actions are available to manage the outcome of rule applications. These actions include:

  • Run: Executes the result function if data satisfies.
  • Confirm: Finalizes the result data, marking it as reviewed and approved.
  • Reject: Discards the result data, indicating that it does not meet the required criteria.
  • Lock Data: Prevents further modifications to the result data, ensuring its integrity.
  • Unlock Data: Reopens the result data for modifications if needed.
  • Import Data: Imports additional data into result table.
  • Export Data: Saves, exports the data that has been provided by execution.
  • Erase Data: Deletes the data in result table.

Rules Maintenance for Allocations

Special adjustments are made for managing allocation rules, ensuring proper handling and display.

  • Settings: The rules rows table separates conditions into two groups: Sender Conditions and Receiver Conditions, along with distribution base.
  • Rule Values: All cells in the ”Settings” section are single dropdowns with options from Source Fields, ensuring clarity and ease of use.
  • Additional Tabs: As Allocation function is dealing with “Sender” and “Receiver” tables, those were added as a separate tabs below the rules maintenance area.

Conclusion

The Dynamic Rules Screen in SAP PaPM Cloud UM significantly enhances the agility and efficiency of business process management. By providing a user-friendly interface for dynamic rule maintenance, and supporting essential features like import/export, undo/redo, version control, commenting, and allocation-specific adjustments, it empowers users to adapt to changing business needs swiftly. Whether you're managing complex business functions or ensuring data consistency, the Dynamic Rules Screen is an invaluable tool for UM Activity users. It is important to note that the Dynamic Rules Screen is applicable only to the functionalities of Allocation, Calculation, Currency Conversion and Derivation, specifically in their processing type versions, not in their model versions (Model Allocation, Model Calculation, Model Derivation).

SAP Managed Tags:

SAP Profitability and Performance Management

entry.title: Re: Unable to build copied app
entry.content:
Hello, thank you for reaching out to us. Could you please try saving it as a new project instead of copying? When you copy a project, the backend needs to be manually redeployed to be accessible as an integration.

here are the more details - https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/save-as-new-project?locale=en-US&q=copying%20apps

entry.title: Re: when I Generate ABAP repository objects.error : Field...
entry.content:
Try to replace the datatype of the field

"createdby" with the dataelement "abp_creation_user" in your database table.

entry.title: Re: How to automate the headers in SAP Disclosure Management
entry.content:
Hello @Srini971 , it's not possible to refresh DM datalink directly in header and footer, however you can use Word bookmark as a workaround. To do that insert named Word bookmark into the header and assign same name to DM floating field with an empty space character behind it - so that once you refresh DM link with the name of the entity required, it will copy over to header.

However, unless you have dozens and dozens of entities, I would rather suggest updating header directly in the report template, it's a quick thing to do.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content: @Shilpa_Shankar Thanks. It's the searching by text that I've struggled with. For textArrangement: #SEPARATE the search help is by technical key, so how to attach the search to the text field. Setting the textArrangement: #TEXT_ONLY shows Status as Text but removes the Technical value from the list:

entry.title: Re: Data is incomplete check message No:F2125
entry.content:
Data is incomplete check message No:F2125(Partner function error)-

SAP SD ERROR TUTORIALS-

"No: F2125 - Partner function error" in SAP usually indicates that there is an issue with the partner function assignment in your document or master data. Partner functions define the roles that business partners play in various transactions, such as a customer being a sold-to party, ship-to party, payer, or bill-to party.

FOLLOW STEPS-

Solution-https://www.learntosap.com/mmerrorpage16.html

 

 

Data is incomplete check message No:F2125(Partner function error)-www.learntosap.com

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano ,

I’m Ahmed, and I’m really excited about the SAP Digital Skills Initiative. With my background in accounting and over 2.5 years as an ERP consultant in the industry, I’ve developed a strong interest in blending technology with business. I’m currently pursuing my ACCA qualification and am eager to transition into a career in SAP, especially as I’m looking to switch careers mid-life.

This program seems like the perfect fit for where I’m headed, and I believe the training and certification it offers will be key in making this career shift successful.

I believe I qualify under the following categories:

  • People looking to switch careers mid-life
  • People in remote or underserved locations that may not have access to the same economic opportunities as those in urban or more privileged areas

I’m looking forward to being a part of this journey and contributing to the SAP community. Thank you for considering my application!

Best regards,
Ahmed

entry.title: CDS view field date element alpha routine
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have a field that displays a document number. For some kinds of document type, document number should be display as it is in the DB, while for some kinds of document type, document number should remove leading zeroes and this can be done by alpha routine in data element.

My question is, can I use different date elements casting against the document type (CASE WHEN)? I tried but looks like only the first date element is working.

Anyone could give some help here?

Regards,

Ming

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Background Processing Framework
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm wondering if there's any way to adjust the user that the background process will be run against. Let's say that a casual user performs an action, and that action must kick off a sophisticated process in the background that the casual user will not authorization to perform.

This would be for Steampunk.

Thanks!

entry.title: Cockpit SAP 7
entry.content:
Dear all, 

As you know, the e-invoice system is implemented in Romania. The company where I'm working has also implemented the Cockpit transaction in SAP, for suplliers , customer end S-fapt. We would like to download the pdf and the XML files created from the eDocument Cockpit app( after the transmission in SPV ) - for a specific period (say the last week)  in order to save them locally. Is this possible/forseen?

How we can save export multiple pdf ( but the E-factura invoice ) and the saving should be done individually, with name and invoice number ? it is not normal to enter each individual invoice( supllier sau customer ) and save it manually... or to the customers after it is sent and authorized by the ANAF , to be automatically downloaded in a signed pdf and xml folder

in the pictures above, it can be seen that the customer's name does not exist. It is possible to add ?What about the supplier? there is no number, there is no VAT (CUI ), name...can it be added?
#E-invoice #Copckpit #export #multiple

entry.title: Mastering ABAP: Unveiling the Power of OOP Concepts of Class & object
entry.content:
Object oriented programing helps in organizing and structuring code in a more manageable way.

Real Time example about OOP


Car is a class. It defines general features like wheels, doors, and methods like start() and stop().A specific car, like a red Toyota Corolla, is an object of this class. Engine is abstracted. You don’t see how it works, just how to start the car. This hides complexity and shows only what’s necessary. The Dashboard encapsulates all controls (speedometer, fuel gauge) in one place, similar to how a class encapsulates data and methods. ElectricCar inherits from the Car class. It has all the features of a car but adds new ones, like a battery. Driving modes like Sport or Eco mode allow the same car to behave differently based on the mode, illustrating how methods can behave differently in different contexts.

 

CLASS AND OBJECT — defines the properties (attributes) and behaviors (methods) that the objects created from the class will have. An object, on the other hand, is an instance of a class that contains actual values for the attributes defined in the class.

Real Time Example

The class represents a group of objects having similar properties and behavior. For example, the animal type Dog is a class while a particular dog named Coffee is an object of the Dog class.

SYNTAX for creating Class

 

 

CLASS <class_name> DEFINITION.
  [PUBLIC|PROTECTED|PRIVATE] SECTION.
    
    DATA: <attribute_name> TYPE <data_type>,
          ...
    METHODS: <method_name> ,
             ...
ENDCLASS.

CLASS <class_name> IMPLEMENTATION.
  "Define methods here
  METHOD <method_name>.
    "Method implementation code
  ENDMETHOD.
  ...
ENDCLASS.

 

 

<class_name>: Name of the class you are creating.
SECTION: Specifies the visibility of the class components (attributes, methods):
PUBLIC SECTION: Accessible from outside the class.
PROTECTED SECTION: Accessible within the class and subclasses.
PRIVATE SECTION: Accessible only within the class itself.
DATA: Used to define attributes (variables) within the class.
METHODS: Used to declare methods (functions) that can be performed by objects of the class.
METHOD: This block is where you implement the logic of the method defined in the class.

 

DATA: <object_name> TYPE REF TO <class_name>.
CREATE OBJECT <object_name>.

 

SYNTAX for creating Object

<object_name>: The name of the object (reference variable) you are creating.
TYPE REF TO <class_name>: Specifies that <object_name> is a reference to an object of type <class_name>.
CREATE OBJECT: This statement instantiates the object.

 

CLASS DEMO1 DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
  METHODS :
  M1.
  DATA: NAME TYPE STRING.
 ENDCLASS.

 CLASS DEMO1 IMPLEMENTATION.
   METHOD M1.
   WRITE 😕 'NAME :', NAME.
   ENDMETHOD.

  ENDCLASS.

  CLASS STATIC DEFINITION.
    PUBLIC SECTION.
  CLASS-DATA : COUNTER TYPE I.
  CLASS-METHODS : COUNT.
  ENDCLASS.

  CLASS STATIC IMPLEMENTATION.
       METHOD COUNT.
     COUNTER += 1.
     WRITE : / COUNTER.
    ENDMETHOD.
    ENDCLASS.

  START-OF-SELECTION.
  DATA: OBJ1 TYPE REF TO DEMO1.
  CREATE OBJECT OBJ1.
  OBJ1->NAME = 'NILA'.
  OBJ1->M1( ).
 STATIC=>COUNT( ).

 

Class DEMO1
Definition:

This class has a method M1 and a data attribute NAME.
The method M1 is declared in the PUBLIC SECTION, meaning it can be accessed from outside the class.
The data attribute NAME is of type STRING, also declared in the PUBLIC SECTION.
Implementation:

The method M1 is implemented to write the text “NAME:” followed by the value of the NAME attribute to the output.
Class STATIC
Definition:

This class has a class-level attribute COUNTER and a class method COUNT.
CLASS-DATA COUNTER: This is a static variable, shared across all instances of the class.
CLASS-METHODS COUNT: This is a static method, which can be called without creating an instance of the class.
Implementation:

The method COUNT increments the COUNTER by 1 and then writes its value to the output.
Main Program (START-OF-SELECTION)
Creating Object OBJ1:

An object OBJ1 of type DEMO1 is created.
The NAME attribute of OBJ1 is set to “NILA”.
The M1 method of OBJ1 is called, which prints “NAME: NILA” to the output.
Calling Static Method COUNT:

The static method COUNT of class STATIC is called directly without creating an object.
This increments the COUNTER by 1 and prints its value.

 

IMPORTANT POINTS TO NOTE

Instance Method vs. Static Method:
M1 is an instance method, meaning it operates on a specific object (OBJ1).
COUNT is a static method, meaning it can be called on the class itself (STATIC=>COUNT( )).
Instance Attribute vs. Static Attribute:
NAME is an instance attribute, meaning each object of DEMO1 has its own NAME.
COUNTER is a static attribute, meaning it is shared by all instances of the STATIC class (though in this example, no instances are created, and it’s accessed directly via the class).

entry.title: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content:
Hi, experts.
Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
Cause i have a task to download document from portal, and there is a specific key ( dongle or hardware key, i don't know how it's called in English ) which allows to enter portal and download documents, so i need to start programm on a specific computer with this dongle.

entry.title: Re: OPEN DATASET syntax for Unicode and non-Unicode, mixe...
entry.content: Unicode

entry.title: Re: OPEN DATASET syntax for Unicode and non-Unicode, mixe...
entry.content:
Hi,

this is a general problem when exchanging char-like data between Non-Unicode and Unicode based systems. It can happen during transport, interfacing (e.g. via RFC) or (as in your case) file exchange.

 Unicode is supporting more than 100000 characters, while code page 1100 (ISO-Latin-1) supports less than 256. As soon as a character outside the Non-Unicode codepage is transferred, the system needs raise an error or to replace it with a replacement character (default in SAP is "#") Alternatively it might be also possible (as already mentioned) to convert the problematic character via transliteration.

However when transferring textual data from Unicode to Non-Unicode, you cannot avoid risk of data loss - this will always be recognized (at the latest) in the receiving Non-Unicode system.

By the way using binary mode is not an option at all - the content will not be readable anymore ...

The best solution would be to use Unicode systems in the landscape only, but this is often not possible in reality ....

Please also have a look at SAP note 1322715 and the attachment of that SAP note called  Requirements_of_ABAP_Programs_in_Unicode_Systems.pdf.

Best regards,

Nils

entry.title: How to install sap Fiori on s/4 hana 2023 fps 01 on embedded system .
entry.content: How to install sap Fiori on s/4 hana 2023 fps 01 on embedded system .

entry.title: Re: Issue with long running PAL Function on HANA
entry.content: In that case, the problem apparently lies within the procedure itself and it is not related to the BW DTP/TRFN framework. I am from BW so we need to wait for someone with experience in this specific HANA procedure.

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "ABAP Core Data Services" developer challenge
entry.content:
Hi,

If you are using BTP Trial system, you cannot add SAP_BR_Developer. Kindly delete the instance and run the Booster to set up ABAP Environment. Download the service key and use it to create a new ABAP Cloud Project. If you remember your package you can add it to the favorites else create a new one. I also noticed that BTP Trial system has less objects than it had earlier. Probably it has been refreshed.

entry.title: Re: How to get the HTML code of a website with SAP Build...
entry.content: Hallo, Ziel des ganzen ist es am Ende auch gar nicht die Website aufzuzeichnen, sondern in einen Prozess die URL einer Website einzugeben und dann den HTML code von der gesamten Website zurückzubekommen. Danach soll dieser dann nach bestimmten Werten durchsucht werden.

entry.title: Re: Experience with Deleting Company Codes Before Go-Live
entry.content: Organizational Structure is unforgiving. In the 2SL once created you cannot delete company codes.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Copy Data to speci...
entry.content:
Hi @guenay,

you need to write your script like follows:

DATA([d/DIMENSION] = [d/DIMENSION].[p/PROPERTY]) 
= RESULTLOOKUP()

I hope this helps
Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2024.001 Versão 1.10: Atualizações recentes na Nota Fiscal Eletrônica (NF-e)
entry.content:

Alguma novidade pra voces? aqui temos dois protocolos, mas ainda sem retorno.

Estou preocupado, so resta uma semana e PR com erro ainda.

Edu

entry.title: XSA Service Instances Migration from On-premise to Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
In the last blog, you learned migration from an on-premise SAP HANA database to an SAP HANA Cloud database instance using the Self-Service Migration for SAP HANA Cloud. Now, will see on how to migrate the XS advanced service instances from on-premise to Cloud Foundry.

Figure: Migration Landscape

Pre-requisites

  • Connectivity Service Entitlement with the service plan “connectivity_proxy” must be enabled for your subaccount using the SAP BTP cockpit.
  • Supported for SAP HANA Database Revision 53 or greater.
  • Source and Tenant databases has XS advanced installed.
  • Migration user in the XSA tenant database of source system with necessary privileges.
  • Establish a connection to the XSA tenant database of source system using Cloud Connector.

Note: Services Instances in default SAP space is not supported for migration.

Procedure

  1. Open SAP HANA Cloud Central, navigate to “Migrations”.
  2. Choose “Create Migration”, specify the migration information and then “Create”.
  3. In the PLAN phase,
  • Enter the virtual host and port that you specified in the cloud connector configuration.
  • Enable Cloud Connector and specify the Cloud Connector Location ID.
  • Enter the credentials of the migration user that was created in the XSA tenant database of the source system.
  • Check Compatibility with SAP HANA Cloud.

  • Tool runs a series of database and application compatibility checks. Once the Self-Service Migration tool finishes checking your database, it lists any incompatibilities found. 
  • Only checks with the severity 'CRITICAL' and 'UNKNOWN' will forbid the migration. Checks with the severity 'ERROR' or below will allow the migration; however, it is possible that some functionality may not work as expected in the target database.
  • Review and resolve the incompatibilities where possible. Choose Proceed to Preparation Phase.

    4. In the PREPARATION phase, provide the following information:

       Migrate XS advanced service instances (SAP HANA Schemas and HDI containers) to Cloud Foundry.

     As the source database has XS advanced installed and the tenant database selected for migration is enabled for XS advanced, the connection to the XS advanced database is configured through the SAP Cloud Connector.

  • Provide the login credentials of the XS advanced administrator user.
  • Configure the connection to the XS advanced runtime through Cloud Connector.
  • Choose Configure Endpoint to generate a configuration file that you  have to upload to SAP Cloud Connector in a later step.

  • Download the Configuration file and upload the same in Cloud Connector. Refer to point 9 in the link for more details.

  • Click on Save and Proceed.

  • Specify the credentials of the SAP BTP technical user.

  • Map the source and target spaces which contain the service instances to be migrated.

  • Specify the credentials of the migration user and Choose Save and Proceed to Select Target.

  • Choose the target database for the migration and enter the credentials of the DBADMIN user on the target database.

  • Confirm all the migration choices and Choose Continue to Execution Phase to proceed to the next phase of the migration.

     5. In the EXECUTION phase, choose “Start Migration” to start the migration process and monitor progress in the status bars. After the migration, a summary is displayed, and you can proceed to the VALIDATION phase.

    6. In the VALIDATION phase, the metadata and data of the migrated content are checked for consistency, also the record count of the tables to be migrated can be checked. Choose “Proceed to Finalization Phase”.

    7. In the FINALIZATION phase, the finalization process removes all artifacts that were temporarily stored by the migration service.Choose “Deactivate the migration user” and “Finalize Migration” to complete the migration and start the post-migration clean-up.

   8. The XS service instances is migrated to Cloud Foundry. A summary screen is displayed, containing information about the completed migration, including statistics about the migrated database, migration step durations, and validation statuses.

   9. “Clean Up Process” will archive the completed migration. Once the migration is archived, it serves as a reference and cannot be altered. Up to 20 migrations can be archived.

 

Hope this blog has helped! For more information refer SAP documentation SAP HANA Cloud Migration Guide.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content: My Solution for Task 3:

entry.title: Crystal Reports take up to 23 sec to show PDF SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi All, 

we are facing the Issue by showing PDF of Documents which are based on CR Reports that these take up to 23 seconds untill the PDF is fully shown. 
The CR Reports are very simple and are just an adjusted Version of the Standard SAP B1 CR Reports, they do not contain any extra complicated queries or so.

meanwhile Documents which are based on PLD report are shown within 2-3 Seconds. 

its a HANA Installation and the SAP Version is 2208

does anyone have this issue? or a solution maybe?

entry.title: Re: Third-Party Keyboards FSM [iOS]
entry.content: Thanks Franzisca for your reply, according to our business team this was working with the previous versions but it's no longer working after updating FSM.

entry.title: Issue with SMS Configuration in Solman
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently receiving alerts via email in Solution Manager, and to enhance the visibility of our systems, we have decided to implement SMS alerts as well. We have configured the SMS settings using our local provider by creating a web service. However, we have encountered an issue: although we can successfully trigger SMS messages manually from SBWP as a test, the SMS messages do not get sent in case of an actual alert.

Could you please provide us with guidance or documentation on how to configure the SMS alerts end-to-end in Solution Manager? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Issue with SMS Configuration in Solman
entry.content: Please review KBA 2489425 - System Monitoring configuration to Send SMS / Text message alerts which provides all required steps to enable System Monitoring notifications via SMS.

entry.title: Re: Fixed Asset Purchase IN S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

Refer to the below link for material purchases with serial number. I think asset one should also work in similar way if material master record exists.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/900eb753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html

Let me know if you are unable to see the field.

Best Regards,

Vijayendra
